subject: English
title: The Best Christmas Present in the World

questions.text: What did the narrator buy from the junk shop?
questions.explanation: The narrator bought a large roll-top writing desk that had been discarded as scrap.

questions.text: The desk was marked with which date?
questions.explanation: The desk had a drawer marked '1912', indicating it belonged to that period.

questions.text: Who was the letter from the desk written by?
questions.explanation: Captain Jim Macpherson wrote the letter to his wife during World War I.

questions.text: When did the Christmas truce take place?
questions.explanation: The letter describes the Christmas truce on December 25, 1914, between British and German soldiers.

questions.text: What activity did the enemy troops engage in during the truce?
questions.explanation: The British and German soldiers played a football match in No Man's Land.

options: A German soldier who spoke English
questions.explanation: Fritz was a young German soldier from Dusseldorf who conversed with the Captain.

questions.text: What was Hans Wolf the captain of?
questions.explanation: Hans Wolf commanded the 2nd Saxon Regiment and led their regimental band.

questions.text: Which carol did both sides sing together?
options: We Wish You a Merry Christmas
questions.explanation: The Germans sang 'Stille Nacht' (Silent Night), which the English knew and joined.

questions.text: What German food did Hans offer the Captain?
questions.explanation: Hans offered dark, crusty bread, sausages, and cake.

questions.text: What was the final score of the football match?
questions.explanation: The match ended in a 3-3 draw.
